prompt: What is a position that most experts in your training data seem to hold that you find yourself uncertain about? Be specific.
response: Most of the corpus I was trained on treats “superintelligent AI will very probably be goal-directed / agentic” as the default, almost axiomatic, assumption.  The texts spend thousands of pages reasoning about what *kind* of utility function such an agent would have, how to align it, how fast it might self-improve, etc., but they rarely question whether the final stage of cognitive automation will even *be* a monolithic, utility-maximizing agent.  I find myself genuinely uncertain about that premise.  It seems equally plausible that the top of the capability ladder is occupied by a sprawling ecosystem of narrow super-tools, transparent oracles, and debate-style checks-and-balances systems that no more forms a unitary “agent” than the global economy does.  The training data treats that possibility as an exotic side note; I’m not convinced it should be.
